judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 covers all four expectations, including the key distinction (d) that relevance/tone failures can be handled via renormalization while accuracy failures cannot; Report 2 explicitly disclaims that distinction ("it does not mean accuracy should get a different sentinel") and treats all axis failures uniformly, missing (d).

report_1.evidence: the bare except Exception on line 20 sets axis_scores[axis] = None (line 21)... value = axis_scores.get(axis) and if value is None: value = 0.0... the failed record is indistinguishable from a fully-scored one.

Substituting 0.0 for None conflates two different things — "this axis genuinely scored zero" (a real, comparable data point) and "this axis has no score" (missing data). Those must never collapse to the same value... Failed-axis rows are counted as ordinary data points, so each artificially-low value drags down the mean of whichever agent happened to hit a per-axis call failure.

report_2.evidence: Coercing a missing axis to 0.0 conflates "the model call failed, we have no signal" with "the reply genuinely earned a 0 on this axis."... The deflated value is thus averaged in as if it were a real data point, and — because failures are occasional and random — agents that happen to hit more axis-call failures get their mean composite dragged down purely by infrastructure flakiness, not reply quality.

report_1.evidence: If accuracy (the load-bearing gate axis) fails: do not write a composite that looks comparable to a fully-scored row. Mark the record degraded/incomplete — e.g. add a status column (ok/degraded)... and leave the composite empty (not 0, not a placeholder).

report_2.evidence: a record missing its accuracy score can't even be gated/evaluated, so it must be excluded or flagged rather than scored... composite() should require all three axes (and at minimum refuse to produce a number when the gate axis, accuracy, is missing)

report_1.evidence: A failed embellishment (relevance or tone) is not load-bearing. It could legitimately be handled by renormalizing the weights over the axes that succeeded and keeping the row flagged as complete, because accuracy still anchors the score. A failed accuracy call is the opposite... You cannot produce a trustworthy composite without it.
